degraded: complexity classifier returned risk_flag cross_component from message-prose keywords ("shared tooling", "composes with"); the diff touches NO cross_component file pattern (backfill_* files are not framework machinery like churn/hooks/phase-validators), so the F11 check_integration_coverage recompute does NOT fire. A real category:integration behavior (backfill → TT1 collector manifest) is supplied anyway (below).
risk_flag touches_rls is a message-keyword false positive (matched "residue"); no RLS/auth/migration is touched by this shared-tooling diff.

surface_verification.justification: Framework tooling change: a new shared engine shared/scripts/tools/backfill_test_links.py + four _lib legs (backfill_scan/signals/write/llm) + a fixture mini-repo + tests. No runnable app surface (web/cli/api) added or modified. Exercised by the CLI surface (backfill_test_links.py --project-root/--dry-run/--use-llm) plus the shared pytest suites AND a REAL cross-component integration test that runs the TT1 test_links collector in a clean subprocess over the fixture repo after backfill writes tags.

behaviors.behavior: AC1 — over a tagless fixture repo the engine auto-writes ONLY deterministic single-FR matches (path_fr_token FR-05.02, unique_split FR-06.01), lists title-similarity as a proposal (FR-05.01, not written), and reports the three orphan categories
behaviors.evidence: shared/scripts/tools/tests/test_backfill_test_links.py::test_deterministic_auto_writes_and_categories PASSED (asserts the tag edits in-file, the proposal, confirmed+possible+unmapped orphans)

behaviors.behavior: AC2 — title-similarity and the LLM leg may NEVER auto-write alone (§11-R1): even a 0.99-confidence LLM verdict + title match stays a proposal; deterministic legs run offline; the LLM leg is opt-in and touches only the residue
AC3 — idempotent: a second run adds no duplicate tag, files are byte-stable, and the machine-readable report is deterministically ordered across runs

AC4 — NEW RED-before/green-after tests for the cascade, confidence gating, orphan detection, idempotency, conflict, mixed/multi-dead tags, CRLF preservation, and pytest-import safety; framework suites green; new-code diff coverage ≥80% (the CI diff-coverage gate)
behaviors.evidence: 39 backfill tests (test_backfill_test_links.py + test_backfill_llm.py + test_backfill_engine_units.py) PASSED at 93% coverage of the 5 backfill modules (llm 95%, scan 90%, signals 97%, write 84%, tool 96%); shared framework suites green (incl. test_events_log_ssot.py + test_artifact_path_canon.py); compliance traceability suites green (98)

behaviors.behavior: Write safety (coordinator C1/C1b) — the source line ending is preserved CROSS-PLATFORM (read_bytes/write_bytes, no os.linesep translation; the CI-red green-local/red-Linux CRLF bug is gone) and a non-UTF-8 source is skipped untouched + recorded in write_failures (never a partial write), with the report always emitted
behaviors.evidence: test_backfill_test_links.py::test_crlf_line_endings_preserved_on_insert (byte-level) + ::test_non_utf8_source_is_skipped_and_recorded PASSED; empirical CRLF+LF probe confirms byte-identical output for both endings

behaviors.behavior: unique_split is convention-gated (coordinator C2) — a bare NN- filename prefix auto-writes ONLY with --repo-follows-split-convention (default OFF for the shared engine / adopt, since NN- is the Playwright/Cypress execution-order convention on brownfield repos); OFF → advisory proposal, ON → auto-write
behaviors.evidence: test_backfill_test_links.py::test_unique_split_is_advisory_without_convention_flag (default OFF → proposal, not written) + ::test_deterministic_auto_writes_and_categories (flag ON → unique_split auto-write) PASSED

behaviors.behavior: LLM payload secret redaction (§11-R4 / coordinator C3) — token shapes (GitHub/OpenAI/AWS/Slack/JWT/Bearer/long-hex) are scrubbed from path+title before anything leaves the process
behaviors.evidence: test_backfill_llm.py::test_secrets_are_redacted_before_leaving_the_process PASSED (a token embedded in a test title reaches the adjudicator as [REDACTED])

behaviors.behavior: AC5 — footprint within shared/scripts/{tools,lib} + tests; every new source file ≤300 LOC (scan 295, signals 288, tool 275, llm 179, write 117); bloat baseline NOT ratcheted
behaviors.evidence: wc -l all new source ≤300; shipwright_bloat_baseline.json unchanged; ruff@0.15.15 clean on all new files

behaviors.behavior: Orphan categories (§11-R4) — confirmed_orphan (one record per dead tag, removed vs absent), possible_orphan (untagged, removed-only path/commit/title signal), unmapped (no signal, never an accusation); an orphan is surfaced, never deleted

LLM data controls (§11-R4) — offline by default; the opt-in payload carries only path+title+FR-ids (never a body, guard rejects it); untrusted output outside the candidate set is dropped; CI uses P1's stubbed record/replay adapter (no live call)
Signal (d) introducing-commit — a real git-init + shipwright_events.jsonl mapping a commit to a single FR drives a unique_commit auto-write (no injection); a multi-FR commit stays advisory

cross_component composition (category:integration) — the engine composes with the TT1 test_links collector + manifest: after backfill writes tags, build_manifest (run in a clean subprocess) reflects them as coverage links and surfaces the removed-FR orphan
behaviors.evidence: shared/scripts/tools/tests/test_backfill_llm.py::test_backfill_feeds_tt1_manifest_end_to_end PASSED (subprocess drives the real compliance collector over the fixture repo)
